title: Variables
learningObjectives: Understand what a variable is and why Python needs them.
Declare, assign, and reassign variables correctly.
Explain Python's dynamic typing and how it differs from static typing.
Read memory diagrams showing how variables are stored in RAM.
Avoid the five most common variable mistakes in Python.
Apply naming conventions (PEP 8) in real code.
Answer common interview questions about Python variables.
history: Variables have been a part of computing since FORTRAN in 1957. Python, created by Guido van Rossum in 1991, adopted a clean, dynamic approach — no type declarations needed. The name 'variable' comes from mathematics, where a symbol can represent changing values.
